headline: Gold worst quarter in 13 years on rate fears
detail: Gold is dumping because the market is pricing in rates staying high longer. High-for-longer rates compress valuations on growth and tech stocks - the exact names you own. CRWD, SMH, SOXL, and QQQ are all sensitive to this; margin amplifies the pain if tech multiples reprice lower.
rec: No new buys triggered by this - but tighten mental alerts on SOXL and SPAL specifically, since leverage plus margin means a rate-driven sell-off hits those positions disproportionately hard. No scanner setup changes the calculus here.

headline: UAE MGX closes $49B AI fund
detail: One of the largest AI-focused funds ever raised signals continued institutional conviction in AI infrastructure. This is broadly supportive of the chip and semiconductor complex - NVDA, SMH, SOXL are direct beneficiaries of sustained AI capital flows. It does not change today's rate-fear picture but is a medium-term tailwind.
rec: No immediate action needed - your SMH and SOXL already give you this exposure. NVDA has a scanner WATCH setup at entry 198.62-197.35 stop, but grade C bias means do not chase; only worth watching if price pulls to that level cleanly.

headline: Oil wobbles as US-Iran talks break down
detail: Oil price instability adds uncertainty to energy costs, which matter to hyperscaler and data center capex - indirectly a soft negative for chip demand expectations. Not an immediate threat but worth monitoring if oil spikes, as it could weigh on tech earnings narratives.
rec: No direct trade warranted from this headline for your current holdings. ERY has a scanner WATCH setup but that is an energy bear ETF - adding a hedge outside your core book on margin would be a distraction unless oil moves materially against you.

headline: LNG disruption may continue for months
detail: Prolonged LNG supply disruption keeps European energy prices elevated, adding to global inflation stickiness. Sticky inflation reinforces the higher-for-longer rate narrative that is already the main risk to your tech-heavy margin book today.
rec: No action needed directly, but this reinforces watching your SOXL and SPAL stops closely - both leveraged positions are first to feel it if the rate-fear trade accelerates into a broader risk-off move.
